_notes: Source: AU Core Practitioner (Australian realm). Target: US Core Practitioner (US realm). Both make name[min=1 MS] and name.family[min=1 MS] mandatory. Key differences: AU Core uses type-discriminator slicing with AU-specific hpii identifier slice; US Core makes identifier mandatory [min=1] with $this-discriminator slicing, mandatory identifier sub-elements (system/value min=1), and NPI slice with error constraints; US Core adds telecom and address with detailed sub-element MS constraints; AU Core marks name.given as MS while US Core does not.

drifts.description: Source root constraints removed in target: AU Core defines inv-pra-0 through inv-pra-4 restricting gender identity, pronouns, and recorded sex or gender extension codings to AU value sets; US Core Practitioner has no equivalent root constraints.
Extension genderIdentity removed in target: AU Core defines Practitioner.extension:genderIdentity (optional, non-MS); US Core Practitioner has no genderIdentity extension slice.
Extension individualPronouns removed in target: AU Core defines Practitioner.extension:individualPronouns (optional, non-MS); US Core Practitioner has no individualPronouns extension slice.
Extension recordedSexOrGender removed in target: AU Core defines Practitioner.extension:recordedSexOrGender (optional, non-MS); US Core Practitioner has no such extension slice.
identifier slicing discriminator changed: source uses pattern:type discriminator (AU hpii slice); target uses pattern:$this discriminator (US NPI slice). Slicing approach changed between realms.

identifier type profile changed: source AU Core allows AU-specific identifier profiles (au-hpii, au-pbsprescribernumber, au-careagencyemployeeidentifier, au-ahpraregistrationnumber) in addition to base Identifier; target US Core uses base Identifier only.
identifier:hpii AU slice [max=1, mustSupport=true, types=Identifier[au-hpii]] removed in target: US Core has no AU identifier slices.

identifier min cardinality strictly increased: target adds identifier[min=1, mustSupport=true]; source AU Core has identifier[mustSupport=true] with no min constraint (effective min=0). Min increased 0→1.

identifier.system min cardinality strictly increased: target adds identifier.system[min=1, max=1, mustSupport=true]; source AU Core has no sub-element constraint on identifier.system (effective min=0). Min increased 0→1.

identifier.value min cardinality strictly increased: target adds identifier.value[min=1, max=1, mustSupport=true]; source AU Core has no sub-element constraint on identifier.value (effective min=0). Min increased 0→1.

New identifier:NPI slice with error constraints added in target: US Core adds identifier:NPI[mustSupport=true] with error constraints us-core-16(error) and us-core-17(error) requiring valid NPI format; source AU Core has no NPI slice.
name.given MustSupport removed on optional element: source AU Core marks name.given[mustSupport=true]; US Core does not constrain name.given in its differential. MS removed on optional element.
telecom MustSupport added on optional element in target: US Core adds telecom[mustSupport=true]; source AU Core does not constrain telecom. MS added on optional element.
telecom.system MustSupport added on optional element in target: US Core adds telecom.system[mustSupport=true]; source AU Core does not constrain telecom.system. MS added on optional element.
telecom.value MustSupport added on optional element in target: US Core adds telecom.value[mustSupport=true]; source AU Core does not constrain telecom.value. MS added on optional element.
address MustSupport added on optional element in target: US Core adds address[mustSupport=true]; source AU Core does not constrain address. MS added on optional element.
address type profile changed: source AU Core uses au-address profile (http://hl7.org.au/fhir/StructureDefinition/au-address) on address; target US Core uses base Address only.
address.line MustSupport added on optional element in target: US Core adds address.line[max=4, mustSupport=true]; source AU Core does not constrain address.line. MS added on optional element.
address.line max cardinality tightened: source [0..*] → target [0..4]. Max decreased from unbounded to 4.
address.city MustSupport added on optional element in target: US Core adds address.city[max=1, mustSupport=true]; source AU Core does not constrain address.city. MS added on optional element.
address.state MustSupport added on optional element in target: US Core adds address.state[max=1, mustSupport=true]; source AU Core does not constrain address.state. MS added on optional element.

address.state new binding added in target: US Core adds extensible(http://hl7.org/fhir/us/core/ValueSet/us-core-usps-state) [US postal state codes]; source AU Core has no binding on address.state. New US realm-specific binding applied.

address.postalCode MustSupport added on optional element in target: US Core adds address.postalCode[max=1, mustSupport=true]; source AU Core does not constrain address.postalCode. MS added on optional element.

address.country MustSupport added on optional element in target: US Core adds address.country[max=1, mustSupport=true]; source AU Core does not constrain address.country. MS added on optional element.

qualification.code binding changed: source preferred(http://terminology.hl7.org.au/ValueSet/v2-0360-extended) [AU extended qualification code VS] → target example(http://terminology.hl7.org/ValueSet/v2-2.7-0360) [R4 base VS]. Binding strength weakened from preferred to example and AU-extended VS replaced.

communication binding changed: source extensible(https://healthterminologies.gov.au/fhir/ValueSet/common-languages-australia-2) [AU common languages VS] → target preferred(http://hl7.org/fhir/ValueSet/languages) [R4 languages VS]. Binding strength changed from extensible to preferred, and AU-specific VS replaced.
